content:
## Summary: Unauthorized Drones Detected in Finnish Airspace

On Sunday morning, Finnish authorities detected multiple low-flying, slow-moving small objects (drones) over sea areas and Southeast Finland. The Air Force scrambled an F/A-18 Hornet fighter jet to identify the objects. Two drones subsequently crashed — one north of Kouvola and one east of Kouvola — with police securing both sites for investigation.

Defense Minister Antti Häkkänen confirmed the incursion, describing it as a serious matter and stating that security authorities responded immediately. The Defence Forces are conducting a joint investigation with other relevant authorities.
